settings.title: Settings
settings.modalIntro: Language, Google Cloud / Sheets help (below), and Canva Connect (OAuth). Your Canva client secret stays only in this desktop app.
settings.hostingIntro: Quick guide to run a local server and share the web app with teammates on the same network.
settings.hostingStep1: Place your Google service account JSON in a local folder (example: ./secrets/service-account.json).
settings.hostingStep2: Export that key into an environment variable (server terminal only).
settings.hostingStep3: Start the web API on all network interfaces.
settings.hostingStep4: Start the web frontend on all interfaces, then share your local IP.
settings.hostingCmdExport: Command (terminal 1) — load JSON into environment variable:
settings.hostingCmdIp: Command (macOS) — get your local IP to share:
settings.hostingOutro: Teammates open http://YOUR_IP:5173 and set API URL to http://YOUR_IP:8787. The Google key stays server-side and is never sent to browsers.

sheetsHelp.title: Google Cloud and Google Sheets
sheetsHelp.modalSubtitle: Read-only access using a service account (JSON key file).
sheetsHelp.intro: To load volunteers and guests from a spreadsheet, connect a Google Cloud project to the Sheets API and a service account.
sheetsHelp.openHelpAria: Help: connect Google Cloud and Google Sheets
sheetsHelp.openHelpTitle: How to connect Google Cloud and Google Sheets
sheetsHelp.step1Desc: In the console, create a project or select an existing one.
sheetsHelp.step2Desc: Under APIs & Services, enable the Google Sheets API for this project.
sheetsHelp.step3Desc: In IAM & Admin → Service Accounts, create an account and download a JSON key.
sheetsHelp.step4Title: Import the key into this app
sheetsHelp.step4Desc: Use “Import JSON” to import the key file; it is copied and stored by the app.
sheetsHelp.step5Title: Create a spreadsheet and get its ID
sheetsHelp.step5Desc: Create or open a Google Sheet. The ID is the segment between /d/ and /edit in the URL.
sheetsHelp.step6Title: Share the spreadsheet with the service account
sheetsHelp.step6Desc: In Share, add the client_email from the JSON (the service account) with Editor access.
sheetsHelp.step7Desc: Enter the spreadsheet ID and tab names, then click “Refresh from Sheets.”

app.tagline: Read-only sync from Google Sheets for volunteers and guests.
app.networkShareDescription: Enable to make the web app reachable by devices on the same Wi-Fi/LAN network.
app.networkShareOffHint: Sharing is off. Enable it to generate a connection link.
app.listMultiHint: Use the checkboxes to open several people at once in the badge illustrator. If none are checked, the selected row is used.
app.emptyList: No people found with this filter.
app.emptyDetailBody: Choose someone in the list to prepare the badge workflow.
app.errorDesktopApi: Desktop API unavailable. Run this app with Electron (`npm run dev`).
app.errorSpreadsheetRequired: Please set Spreadsheet ID and import the Service Account JSON first.
app.errorImportServiceAccount: Could not import the service account JSON.
app.errorImportDesktopOnly: JSON import is available only in the desktop app.
app.errorWebApiRequired: Set the Web API URL to use the web version.
app.errorNetworkShareUnavailable: Network sharing is unavailable in this session. Restart the desktop app after updating.
app.errorLoadSheets: Failed to load Google Sheets data.

canva.headerHint: Connect API (OAuth). Your client secret stays only in this desktop app — it is never sent to the web UI bundle.
canva.mfaBody: If you don’t see MFA options in your Canva account settings, try resetting your password in Canva’s account settings — after that, MFA controls often become visible so you can finish securing your account for API / developer use.
canva.step1Li2: Create a new integration and choose the <ca>Connect API</ca> type (or open the <ca>Connect API documentation</ca>).
canva.step1Li3Prefix: In the integration’s Authentication (or similar) section, add this redirect URL exactly — copy/paste with no extra spaces:
canva.step1PortHint: This app listens on 127.0.0.1 port 32887 only during “Connect to Canva”. Keep this port free or the login step will fail.
canva.step1Li4: Under Scopes, enable at least: design:content:write, design:meta:read, brandtemplate:meta:read, brandtemplate:content:read, asset:read, asset:write. Save the integration.
canva.step1Li5: Copy the integration’s Client ID and Client secret (starts with cnvca) — you’ll paste them in step 2 below.
canva.step1AltHint: Alternative: set environment variables CANVA_CLIENT_ID and CANVA_CLIENT_SECRET when launching the app (no need to use the fields below). After changing scopes, use Disconnect then Connect to Canva again so the new scopes are granted.
canva.step1AutofillHint: Autofill requires a Canva Enterprise org (or developer access approved by Canva). See the <link>Autofill guide</link> on canva.dev.
canva.step2Title: 2. Save API credentials in this app
canva.step2Li1: Paste Client ID and Client secret from the portal.
canva.step2Li2: Click Save credentials locally. They are stored in your app profile folder on disk.
canva.step2Li3: To update only the Client ID later, paste the new ID and leave the secret blank, then save (the saved secret is kept).
canva.placeholderSecretKeep: Leave blank to keep saved secret
canva.placeholderBrandTemplate: e.g. AEN3TrQftXo — from your published brand template URL
canva.brandTemplateHint: Create a brand template with the Data autofill app using field names from docs/CANVA_BRAND_TEMPLATE.md. Optional env: CANVA_BRAND_TEMPLATE_ID. You can save this field alone if API keys are set via environment variables.
canva.statusBrandNo: · no template ID — Send to Canva uses PDF import (flattened)
canva.step3Li1: Click Connect to Canva (browser). Your default browser opens Canva’s consent screen.
canva.step3Li2: Approve access for this integration. You’ll be redirected to a small local page saying “Connected”.
canva.step3Li3: Return to this app — status should show Connected to Canva.
canva.disconnectHint: Disconnect removes saved login tokens from this app only; your Client ID / secret in step 2 are kept until you overwrite them.
canva.msgNoApi: Desktop API unavailable. Run with Electron (`npm run dev`).

illustrator.title: Badge illustrator
illustrator.subtitle: Select a side and configure overlays.
illustrator.frontHint: Design is fixed to your approved layout. The cover image is bundled in the app (src/assets/logo). Only the QR code and NFC mark are configurable.
illustrator.qrSectionHint: Anchor (top / right / width) plus fine X, Y, Z and zoom. Design-pixel X/Y match the logo sliders.
illustrator.backHint: Configure person type, name, photo, logos and QR code for the back side.
illustrator.setPersonTypeDefault: Set person type & accent as default
illustrator.roleEdgeHint: Saved per person type. Positive moves the vertical role toward the outer edge of the card; negative toward the centre.
illustrator.nameHint: Prefilled from the sheet for this person. Not saved as global defaults.
illustrator.secondaryHint: Used by venue logos, last name and back QR code. Default: white.
illustrator.backQrHint: Encodes EventManagerApp JSON from sheet data only. Volunteers use id + sheetsId; guests use name + abbr. Temporary guests do not get a QR code.

export.zipPdfSaved: ZIP saved with {{count}} PDFs (front + back per person).
export.canvaNeedsDesktop: Send to Canva requires the desktop app (Electron).
export.canvaConfigureSettings: Configure Canva in Settings (gear) or set CANVA_CLIENT_ID / CANVA_CLIENT_SECRET.
export.canvaConnectAccount: Connect your Canva account: Settings (gear) → Connect to Canva.
export.canvaAutofillUnavailable: Send to Canva (Autofill) is unavailable. Update the desktop app.
export.canvaPreparingLayers: Preparing text and images for Canva…
export.canvaOpenedLayers: Opened Canva — text and images are editable layers.
export.canvaOpenedBatchPdf: Opened Canva with {{count}} two-page PDF imports.
export.canvaOpenedPdfFallback: Opened Canva with your badge (2-page import — add a brand template ID for editable layers).
export.bsBatchDone: Badgy Studio batch (.bs): {{count}} people — use the database panel in Badgy to switch between cards.
export.bsSingleDone: Badgy Studio project exported (.bs) — front + back embedded as backgrounds.
export.rasterBatchDone: {{format}} batch: ZIP with front and back files for {{count}} people.
export.rasterSingleDone: {{format}} exported for both sides as ZIP.
export.readmePdfBatch:
One PDF per person (2 pages each: front then back).
Filenames match each person’s badge name.
export.readmeRasterBatch:
Per person: `<name>_front.{{ext}}` and `<name>_back.{{ext}}`.
Generated from the badge illustrator.
export.readmeRasterSingle:
This archive contains both sides of the badge export.
Front and back files are generated from the live preview.
